titleStmt.title: SII 9.289: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 289. (A.R. No. 506 of 1915.) ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT KARAKANTHAPURA, A HAMLET OF PEDDATUMBALAM, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: The stone on which the record is incised is broken into pieces and only two pieces are found. The inscription has no date but refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and gives the boundary of some land, evidently a religious gift.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī . namastuṁgasiraccuṁbicaṁdracāmaracā
ravē . traiḷōkyanagarārambhamūlastaṁbhāya
saṁbhavē . pāṁtu vō jaladasyāmāḥ śāṁrgajyā
ghātakarkkaśāḥ . trailōkyamaṇṭapastaṁbhāśca
tvārō haribāhavaḥ . svasti samasta
bhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārā
jādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭāraka satyā
śrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīma
tṛbhuvanamalladēvara vijayarājyamuttarō
ttarābhi vri mācaṁdrārkkatāThe stone is mutilated.

Ādityabhaṭṭōpādhyāyara holadiṁdaṁ teṁkalu ha
ḍuvalādavaniya dāri mēre teṁkalu maṇiku
ṟuvina holavēre mēre mūḍalu hiriyaha
ḷḷaṁ mēre Antu kisumogesammiśra mattaru 75
noṟunavileṟa Ūra horeyaṇa ha
dakāluveya gaddeyma raca
ḷiya bāḍaṁ ba The stone is
Digital edition of SII 9.289 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
